entry.title: Re: Difference between ABAP Central Instance before and after SAP Netweaver 7.3
entry.content:
Hello, May be this is a silly question but does below sentence means 

"ASCS  Instance has been split off from Central Instance." 

Central Instance is separate component in a part form PAS and ASCS in new SAP version?

entry.title: How many days it takes to decide the organisation structure of the company
entry.content: Usually how many days it takes to decide organisation structure of the company.

entry.title: 1H 2024 Release Highlights of SAP Best Practices for SAP SuccessFactors Time Management
entry.content:
Dear All,

 

Hope you are doing fine.

 

I am excited to share the new features that we are planning to deliver as part of the Best Practices package for Time Management 1H 2024 via the upgrade center. We have some new features (new country localizations) along with enhancements for existing countries. We actually delivered a lot of new features in this release, and this is what makes it exciting for those of you who are looking for any accelerated implementations

 

Disclaimer: Please note these features are currently available only on preview. The final version will be updated on RTC or production date for 1H 2024.

 

Time OFF

 

Finland Localization

 

We now provide localized configuration for Finland (FIN) Time Off. By applying the country-specific configuration block, you will get sample configuration for various leave types like Vacations, Illness, Child Care, Study, Unpaid Absence, Birth of Child, Military Reserve, Parental Allowance etc

 

Finland has complex vacation laws and lot of quotas which have to be handled via different time account types. In phase 1, we deliver baseline configurations to cater to 14 days rule. The 14 day rule covers those who work for at least 14 days every month under their contract. They accrue holiday for each calendar month when they have accumulated at least 14 days at work or the equivalent of 14 days at work

 

 Here are the different time accounts and their terminologies.

First the Holiday credit year or account gets monthly accruals of 2 /2.5 days depending on the employee service period. Next the accruals are transferred from Holiday Credit year to Vacation account at the end of holiday credit period

The below are some of the examples that demonstrate this

 

 

Some more examples of time account and accrual process (movement between different accounts)

 

 

 

Further certain leave types impact the monthly accruals. These leave types have individual limits. Any working days and working hours when the employee has been unable to work for the reasons set out below are considered to be the equivalent of days at work:


1)Pregnancy Leave: The limit is 40 days and days counted from Monday to Saturday. The absence is counted as working days which accrues holiday quotas.
2) Parental Allowance Leave. The absence can also be a part day and in several periods. Limit is 160 + 160 = 320 days for one child and days counted from Monday to Saturday. 160 days of the absence is counted as working days. Only 160 days will accrue holiday quotas
2) Illness or accident : the maximum is 75 working days in one vacation year;
3) Medical rehabilitation : the maximum is 75 working days in one vacation year
4) Study leave, maximum is 30 working days in one vacation year, and only if the employee has returned to work immediately after the study leave;
5) Lay-offs,  the maximum is 30 working days at a time; Only one layoff event is supported for now

Till the above limits is not breached, the number of working days is not reduced for that month (for 14-day rule calculation of accruals)

 

To check the detailed configurations, you can choose to upgrade the content using upgrade center.

 

 

 

IRELAND Localization

 

We now provide localized configuration for Ireland (IRE) Time Off. By applying the country-specific configuration block, you will get sample configuration for various leave types like Annual Leave, Give Time Leave , Sickness, Maternity, Paternity , Adoption, Sabbatical , emergency ,family care etc

 

Some key points:

  • Yearly accruals of 26 days with proration logic for Hiring and Termination cases
  • Give Time Leave of 3 days for personal purposes
  • Complex validations to check different leave limits across a rolling period of 1 year for emergency and family care leaves.
  • Sabbatical leave – Employee should complete 1 year in current position and 4 years in the organization
  • Parental Leave – Parental leave lets parents take unpaid leave from work to spend time looking after their children. You can take up to 26 weeks’ parental leave for each eligible child before their 12th birthday. Since this is a custom solution, we have not included this in Best practices package as such , but if you are interested to explore this, you can read my blog below to decipher the custom approach

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/parental-leave-ireland-solution-proposal-in-successfactors-time-management/ba-p/13579269

 

This solution is not just restricted for Ireland but can be applied to any country with similar requirements.

 

To check the detailed configurations, you can choose to upgrade the content using upgrade center (Follow the same approach as Finland listed above)

          

 

Oman Time Off  Enhancements

 

We did some major changes for OMAN in 2405  w.r.t some new labor laws that were enforced from 2024.

IF you want to deploy them, please do review them to ensure that they meet your specific requirements.

The below are some of the key changes:

  1. Caregiver leave: 15 days per year are given to Omani workers to accompany a patient they are related to up to second degree. 
  2. Paternity leave: 7 days, provided that the child is born alive, and the father takes this leave within 98 days (about 3 months) of the child's birth
  3. Bereavement leave: taken by a non-Muslim female for 14 days in the event of the death of her husband. 
  4. Childcare leave: Unpaid leave for a female worker for up to 1 year; provided that the employee bears all contributions to the Social Protection Fund
  5. Special Unpaid leave: An indefinite period of unpaid leave, provided that the employee bears the cost of all contributions to the social protection fund during the leave period
  6. Nursing break: 1 hour per day for a nursing worker from the end of maternity leave for a period of 1 year
  7. Maternity leave: has increased from 50 days to 98 days to cover the period before and after childbirth. It can be taken 14 days before the delivery date
  8. Sick leave: total annual sick leave has increased from 10 weeks or 70 days to 182 days and employees shall be entitled to:

 

  • The first 21 days, full pay (100%).
  • From day 22-35, at 75% pay.
  • From day 36-70, at 50% pay.
  • From day 71-182 at 35% pay.

     9. Bereavement leave: in the event of the death of a wife or child; extended from 3 to 10 days.

 

Again if you want to take an advantage of accelerated deployment, you can deploy them using upgrade center as highlighted for Finland already above

 

 

Time Forms Content

 

Companies may need to provide employees with official documents detailing the information kept in their Time Management data along with employment and personal details.

This SAP Best Practices for Time Management now provides the following common configuration:

Time Form Templates

  •              Holiday Letter Example
  •              Maternity Leave Example
  •              Paid Annual Leave Example
  •              Strike Letter Example

And top also Role-Based Permissions for Administrators to manage time form templates and generate time forms.

With this, you don’t have to set these templates manually from scratch. It saves a lot of efforts and cost to use and deploy them. Please note this requires document generation to be enabled as well.

Here are few examples of some generated sample documents.

Maternity letter

 

 

 

Paid Annual Leave

 

 

 

These predefined templates and configuration is also part of our Upgrade center automation. You could use the below upgrade to apply these changes and benefit from a quick accelerated implementation

 

 

 

 

Enhanced Absence Counting Rules ( Singapore)

When an employee applies leave that includes days in which only half day working is planned depending on the work schedule , the deduction quantity should be 0.5 in the corresponding time account (Considering time type unit is days here).

 

Example:

 

 

In this case a total of 4.5 days is deducted for a week of absence.

 

The best practices package for Time Off Singapore comes with preconfigured absence counting rules to tackle above requirements, and you don’t have to explicitly implement them from scratch

You could use the upgrade center to deploy the above config for Singapore.

Additionally, If you are also using package configuration manager for building your packages, you could also deploy the absence counting 2.0 sample rules from PCM

 

 

Time Tracking

 

United Kingdom Localization

 

This comes up with a preconfigured time valuation rules for United Kingdom to handle Overtime rules for weekdays, holidays, Saturdays and Sundays for part timers and full time employees.

This is just the phase 1 of localization. We plan to extend this package in future with more region-specific requirements.

In UK, we have a specific requirement around part timers which is kind of unique to UK and European regions. There is the regulation that part time employees are entitled for an overtime premium only when they have reached the full-time equivalent regular hours. 

For example:

Part time employee will get the Overtime @1.0  for additional hours worked upto the FTE hours i.e 40 hours per week . Any hours over and beyond FTE hours will be paid at the same rate as the full time employee. Part time employees will get the weekend premium rate irrespective of if they have completed the FTE hours or not.

Employee planned working time = 30 hours/Week
Total working time submitted and approved = 45 hours
Overtime hours = 15 hours
FTE threshold hours = 40 Hours/Week
Calculation:
Delta hours to ensure completion upto FTE Hours = FTE hours(40) - planned hours(30) = 10 hours
Total overtime hours = 15 hours
First 10 hours i.e delta will be paid at the overtime@1.0 and the remaining (15-10) 5 hours will be paid at overtime1.5
Result: OT@1.0 = 10 hours and OT@1.5 = 5 hours

 

You could avail this configuration using UK specific time tracking package in upgrade center

 

South Korea Localization

 

This comes up with a preconfigured time valuation rules for South Korea to handle Overtime and Cross Midnight premium payouts based on the below schedule and rules

 

 

 

You could avail this configuration using Korea specific time tracking package in upgrade center

 

 

Flexible Period Time Valuations

 

We delivered a sample time recording profile which can track overtime using flexible time valuation periods like a yearly overtime. For example: Alerts are generated if employee breaches a limit of 220 overtime hours in a year. This is using the concept of time containers.

This was also patched as part of 2311. This is just FYI in case you are not aware of this.

This particular feature is part of Time Tracking Core Setup which means this can be applied in general for any country.

 

This is it w.r.t the enhancements and new features that we introduced in 1H 2405 for time management best practices. I hope this adds value in accelerating any implementations that you have.

For more information on best practices, please do visit our help page in

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BEST_PRACTICES_FOR_HXM

 

Thanks

Neelesh

Senior Product Manager

SAP Best Practices For HCM SuccessFactors

entry.title: Re: TR is imported into production but status in Solman is still authorized for imported
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: Feature Request: Show which processes are using a cer...
entry.content:
Dear Anna, 

many thanks for your feedback. I have forwarded it to the responsible product owner and added it to our feature requests. 

Kind regards, 
Cäcilia

entry.title: Re: HU information not getting updated in SAP TM for Inbo...
entry.content: Hi Johnny,

entry.title: Re: Error in ABAP CDS View for annotation @Analytics.quer...
entry.content:
To create a model using CDS views in SAC, analytics.query: true annotation is not alone enough.

You also want to publish OData service in SAP GUI. And add Annotation, OData.pulish: true.

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit for migrate from SAP TM Cloud extra...
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit provides migration object to SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • from any legacy systems
  • from SAP ECC systems.

Please check list of objects here:

http://help.sap.com/S4_CE_MO 

Regards,

Sybille Lackermeier

entry.title: Re: SIGNAVIO Governance: Start a workflow from dictionary...
entry.content:
Good day Tiago, 

thank you for your question. It is not possible to start your desired workflow from the Process Manager, but you can built such an integration with JavaScript. Our consulting team has done this in the past for other customers.

If you'd like to gain more insights, on how it is working, and how much time it takes, please contact your Sales person to get connected to our consulting team. 

If you do not know your Sales contact, you can also reach out to signavio_success@sap.com and we will connect you with your sales contact. 

Kind regards, 
Cäcilia

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge - APIs - Task 7 - Create a new directory in an SAP BTP account
Re: SAP Asset Manager official app - Offline maps - permi...
entry.content:
Hi,

On Android 10 and above, you don't have to ask for granting file permission. Permissions of App scoped files will be granted automatically. However, if you want to access files out of your app sandbox, that will be challenging. You will need to access that file created by other app via ContentProvider

 

Reference: https://developer.android.com/training/data-storage/shared/media#storage-permission-not-always-needed

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting requirements in MRP area
entry.content: Hi, Thank you for your reply. I have went through the note 2551191, now that I understand it is standard behavior that quota is not consider under subcontracting MRP area. In that case, what could be the suggested solution if quota arrangement should not be ignore?

entry.title: RPA SMTP Mail Destination Configuration with OAuth2Password for Microsoft 365?
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

Have anyone succesfully manage to configure SMTP mail server with OAuth2Password base on the information below? This is for microsoft 365 account. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configuring-smtp-mail-destination

I've registered the app within Entra ID and give the correct permission (base on my current assumptiong). Testing the scope, client id and secret via postman seems to be okay (it returns the token). 

However testing within BPA lobby is showing authentication issue. I'm trying to figure out if there is a missing configuration or is there something wrong within the test mail function? 

#sbpa #irpa #bpa #automation

entry.title: Re: Migrate Your Data - MARD_PSTAT missing "D"
entry.content:
Hi Steven,

this this was caused by simplification in the material master management, the resolution is provided in" SAP note “3410186 - Message "No storage-location-specific data exists" in MRP view.

Tomas

entry.title: Re: Can we trigger workflow from custom program even if w...
entry.content:
Hello @anjalihora 

You can easily create any Workflow even for a custom program - see SAP Help:

  • Creating Events by Calling a Function Module
  • Creation of Events by Using ABAP Classes

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with Spaces and Pages
entry.content: Hi George, thanks for your comment. Happy you like them. Cheers, Kattia

entry.title: Origin group in overheads
entry.content:
Hello,

While doing ECP for projects, is this possible to populate origin group for item category G ( planned overheads)? I tried assigning origin group for cost component ID associated with overheads but still it does not populate origin group when ECP is loaded. When I manually try to add any details including origin group in line item for item category G, system just wipes it out after pop up (incorrect details are deleted). Can we say that SAP does not allow any origin group for planned overheads in ECP ( for item category G) and it will be always blank. I did not find any relevant SAP material on this subject. Kindly provide your valuable inputs.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Origin group in ECP
entry.content: Closing this thread.

entry.title: Re: IDOC for order confirmation and GR posting in background
entry.content: thanks for your answer

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hello community! I am excited to join this group.

entry.title: im getting an error while posting GL document as GL102(Check the doc as well as tableT001&T880
entry.content:
Global company MAXIND is not supported

Message No. GI102

Diagnosis

The direct posting function could not find the global company specified. The global company has either been entered in the document or read from table T001 based on the transferred company code.  Each global company must be defined in table T880.

System Response

Direct posting to General Ledger cannot take place.

Procedure

Check the document as well as tables T001 and T880.

entry.title: Re: Update TRDAT of LOGON DATA
entry.content: If you want to change anything about security, you should contact SAP Consulting.

entry.title: Re: How many days it takes to decide the organisation str...
entry.content:
Hi,

Deciding the Organizational structure for company can take weeks to months, depending on how big and complicated the company is, how involved everyone is, and if any special changes are needed.

Regards

entry.title: Re: set varian transaction QA11
entry.content: Is there any issue, what is it?

entry.title: Unlock the Future of Clean Core at SAP Sapphire 2024 Orlando!
entry.content:
The Clean Core Revolution

In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, maintaining a clean core has never been more crucial. A clean core strategy empowers organizations to streamline operations, enhance agility, and drive innovation by focusing on simplifying and optimizing core business processes. At SAP Sapphire 2024 in Orlando from June 3-5, 2024, we're diving deep into the transformative power of a clean core and how it can be a game-changer for your business.

Why You Can't Miss SAP Sapphire 2024

Get ready to embark on an inspiring journey of innovation and transformation at SAP Sapphire 2024 in Orlando from June 3-5, 2024! This premier event offers a unique opportunity to connect with industry experts, explore cutting-edge solutions, and gain insights into the latest trends shaping the future of business technology.

Spotlight on Clean Core: Don't Miss These Must-Attend Sessions!

  • Session 1: Enable a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM (SER253)
    Discover SAP's strategy for RISE with SAP Migration and Modernization, with a focus on SAP Cloud ALM as a platform for a clean core. Understand how to fortify your competitive edge by streamlining complexity, separating standard extensions, and managing data following the most recent standards. Uncover methods to preserve a reliable, adaptable infrastructure while retaining operational productivity and competence.

  • Session 2: Start Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Journey Fresh with a Clean Core Strategy (SER258)
    Ready to start your SAP S/4HANA Cloud journey, be it greenfield or brownfield? Explore how embedded launch activities can support a clean-core strategy, guiding you through crucial initial steps. These activities, included in your subscription, optimize processes at no extra cost, supporting your cloud ERP transformation, regardless of approach.

Exclusive Opportunity for 1:1 Meetings with Clean Core Experts!

Don't miss the chance to engage directly with our Clean Core experts during SAP Sapphire 2024! We're offering limited opportunities for 1:1 meetings at the Services and Support Center on the show floor in Orlando.

Limited Availability!
Take advantage of this unique opportunity to discuss your specific challenges and explore tailored solutions with our experts. Secure your 1:1 appointment now before slots fill up!

Request your meeting here: 👉 I want to talk Clean Core at SAP Sapphire 2024 Orlando

Get Ready to Elevate Your Clean Core Strategy!

Join us at SAP Sapphire 2024 in Orlando to discover the power of a clean core and how it can transform your business operations. Engage with industry leaders, attend insightful sessions, and take the first step towards building a future-ready, efficient, and resilient business.

We look forward to seeing you there!

Feel free to share this article to promote the Clean Core sessions at SAP Sapphire 2024 Orlando and encourage your network to arrange 1:1 meetings with our experts!

entry.title: Re: I have a Enhancement for F3302 App
entry.content: hi, what is your BAS run configuration? Anyhow, copy the SAP standard is not a good approach, as you will not get the latest feature/correction from SAP. So you should do it via extension: 1. metadata extension - you missing @Consumption.filter; VH annotation, as you already added, 2. SEGW project extension - to expose that VH CDS view entity to OData service.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out to express my genuine interest in acquiring a free certification opportunity in Designing Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud. As an Analyst in India, I recently completed a learning journey focused on Configuring SAP SuccessFactors People Analytics. With 0.8 years of professional experience, I aim to further refine my skills through formal certification.

In addition to the mentioned learning journey, I've pursued various relevant educational endeavors, strengthening my foundation for excelling in this certification.

As a first-generation learner, I deeply value continuous learning and professional growth. Acquiring this certification would not only validate my competencies but also foster personal and career advancement. I am committed to investing time and effort into expanding my skill set, knowing this certification will significantly contribute to my professional journey.

I am confident that the knowledge and insights gained from this certification will enable me to make meaningful contributions to my current and future projects.

Recognizing the significance of certifications in today's competitive landscape, I appreciate the opportunity to apply for this program. I assure you of my dedication and readiness to leverage this opportunity to its fullest potential.

Thank you for considering my application. I eagerly await further details regarding the process. Kindly inform me if any additional information are required.

SAP Community screenname - Pradipta

Looking forward to your positive response 

Thanks and regards,

Pradipta Satpathy

entry.title: Create New Controlling Area in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi There,

We need additional controlling areas and it needs to be mapped to existing company codes, how to create in public cloud?

Thank you,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Create New Controlling Area in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud there is only one controlling area (A000). This configuration cannot be changed under any circumstances.

Help Portal: Controlling Area

Maintain Controlling Area in CBC - S/4HANA Cloud

Regards,

Ankit K

entry.title: Re: Risk is not appearing in Risk analysis Screen
entry.content: Please any one reply regarding the above issue

entry.title: Katowice - SAP Stammtisch 26.06.2024
entry.content:
The Local SAP Community in Katowice meets soon.

 

SAP Stammtisch in Katowice will happen on June 26th, 2024 at 6 PM CET, so join us and be a part of our Group!

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please note that the location has been changed to a summer bar by the pond in the Dolina Trzech Stawów!

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", which will allow us to reserve the right number of places at our meeting point.

Find more information in the blog posts board: blog-posts 

and we also invite you join the Katowice Group if you are not yet the member: SAP Community Katowice

entry.title: Re: SAP Alert Notification Service : Send Notifications to E-mail, Microsoft teams
entry.content:
Hi Dirugadr,

As far as I know, a single event should be able send an alert to up to 100 recipients, but recommendation is really to create an email group or distribution list on your side. The benefits are that just one confirmation is needed in order to activate the action type for all recipients (so you prevent that each recipient will have to confirm reception of corresponding notification emails) and that you can deliver notifications to more than 100 recipients. Please also see this question: Adding Distribution List in Alert Notification Ser... - SAP Community

Best regards,
Boris

entry.title: EPPM-项目经理权限控制ⅠⅠ
entry.content:
系统现状

当使用权限企业项目-项目经理角色（SAP_PROJECT_MANAGER），同时又需要项目分析报表权限在角色业务目录里增加业务目录企业项目-项目财务分析(SAP_PS_BC_PROJ_FIN_ANLYTC_MC)

会使APP：我的项目-项目经理中，项目概要显示所有项目经理的项目

Workaround：通过以下设置可以恢复只显示项目经理为自己的项目

1.APP：维护业务角色，对角色Z_BR_PROJECTMANAGER维护限制

  • 设置写入访问和读取访问为受限制
  • 对页签S4PPM_PR1设置公司代码为9999（任何非真实公司存在代码都可以）
  • 对其他所有页签和字段设置为无限制

2.再次打开APP：我的项目-项目经理，项目概要只显示项目经理为自己的项目

 

感谢观看

entry.title: Re: im getting an error while posting GL document as GL10...
entry.content:
Hi,

Goto OX16 check Company Code assignment to company.

then OX15 define the company if missing/deleted and assign it to the company code.

Regards

entry.title: Re: CSRF Token Validation Error
entry.content: @LuizGomes are you sure that the correct route is used?

entry.title: Solution Options for Production Planning and Scheduling
entry.content:
Summary

In this article we describe alternative SAP application landscape options for Production Planning and Scheduling and provide guidance on deciding the most suitable target application architecture. The primary focus is Constraint-based Planning and Scheduling. For MRP related options please refer to this article: Solution Options for Material Requirements Planning. 
A list of criteria is intended to help evaluating the options to make the right choice for a customer, taking their situation and preferences into account.

Business Requirements

The business requires the ability to plan and schedule production taking capacity and other constrains into account.

  • Ability to perform detailed planning and scheduling, which enables constraint-based planning through scheduling and sequencing.
  • Optimize Production Planning and Constraints Management and plan production resources finitely beyond S/4HANA MRP functionality (including live MRP, DDMRP).
  • Create production plans and schedules based on business sales, operations, and inventory plans with instant feedback loops from manufacturing and logistics execution.

According to SAP Reference Business Architecture the Manufacturing Performance Management business area comprises the following capabilities (Figure 1 - available to view in SAP Signavio Process Explorer)


Figure 1 – Manufacturing Performance Management Business Capabilities 

Business Capabilities and Process Alignment

From the “Plan to Fulfill” end-to-end business process you can drill down to the activities enabled by the business capabilities (Figure 2):


Figure 2 – Business Process Module – Activities - Capabilities

The Solution Value Flow Diagram (Figure 3) shows the implementation of a Solution Process Module by depicting an implementable combination of Solution Components and their corresponding Solution Capabilities for hybrid deployment.


Figure 3 – Solution Value flow
 

Solution Options

The following SAP solutions are available to enable Production Planning and Scheduling capabilities (see Figure 3 above).

  • SAP S/4HANA Enterprise core – basic production planning capabilities:
    - Material Requirements Planning (MRP)
    - Capacity Requirements Planning (CRP)

This solution option is not discussed further in this article – for architecture decision please refer to Solution Options for Material Requirements Planning.

  • SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling (MP&S, aka Advanced Planning)
  • SAP Synchronized Planning for Production (SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) + S/4HANA MP&S)

SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for Planning and Scheduling (MP&S) is often referred as Advanced Planning or S/4HANA PP/DS as well.
Note that PP/DS (or PPDS) is a function module in SAP APO while S/4HANA has the embedded PP/DS. In this article we will use MP&S as the broader end-to-end solution and refer to the PP/DS module of S/4HANA where appropriate (S/4HANA PP/DS).  

Note: Along with the Business Suite, SAP APO mainstream maintenance is going to end by 12/31/2027. Extended maintenance is available until 12/31/2030. For further details please refer to SAP Maintenance Strategy: Maintenance 2040, Product Availability Matrix (PAM), SAP note 1648480.
Moving forward, the platform of innovation for production planning is SAP Integrated Business Planning and SAP S/4HANA.

 

Characterization of the Solution Options

The brief description below focuses on the production planning capabilities of SAP S/4HANA and SAP IBP only and is not intended to provide the full feature scope of the products.

SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for Planning and Scheduling

SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling (aka PPDS) is an integral part of the end-to-end planning capability/suite from SAP and allows the creation of feasible production plans and schedules that optimize scarce resources to stabilize production and help making reliable commitments to internal and external customers. This S/4HANA Enterprise Management core extension, available both on premise and as private cloud edition, in stack and extra stack (side by side as S/4HANA DSC-Edition), ensures that planning and execution are synchronized in real time.
SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling is a solution for planning production resources and considering finite capacity and material availability constraints, the full APS-functionality directly embedded in S/4 (APS: Advanced Planning and Scheduling).

Key capabilities include:

  • Time-continuous scheduling (i.e., down to the second), supporting features like alternative resources, min/max constraints, dynamic setup times and costs
  • Multi-level pegging (i.e., material flow), connecting demand and supply elements
  • Alerting enabling exception-based planning
  • Advanced Planning & Scheduling Optimization Solvers and Heuristics
  • Simulation versions and transactional simulation
  • Industry specific functionality (Block Planning, Campaign Planning, Shelf-Life …)
  • Web based Scheduling Board aka. “Gantt Chart” (Fiori app)
  • Optimize Production Planning and Constraints Management and plan production resources finitely beyond classic MRP functionality
  • Consider capacity and material availability constraints in the planning model
  • Integrate manufacturing master data to define the basis for production planning data structures
  • Streamline scheduling processes throughout global production environments
  • Perform detailed scheduling with a setup matrix, sequence constraints, and alternative modes

 

Deployment options

As mentioned above, S/4HANA PP/DS is available as embedded or “side car” deployment (Figure 4). Technically both are the embedded PP/DS in S/4HANA, but the SAP DSC Edition is limited to supply chain functionalities (see Side by side deployment below) and other S/4HANA modules/functionalities are not available.


Figure 4 – SAP S/4HANA MP&S (PP/DS) deployment options

Embedded PP/DS

SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling (embedded PP/DS) is the preferred option.

  • Simplified system landscape
  • Leaner integrations between ERP core and advanced layer of SAP S/4HANA
  • But implies dependency for the move from ERP to SAP S/4HANA

Side by side deployment

This deployment option is based on SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, Edition for SAP S/4HANA (S/4HANA DSC-Edition) which is an S/4HANA core with only the below embedded solutions – no other S/4HANA functionalities are available:

The side car deployment is a valid option to accelerate the supply chain transformation or for hub scenarios. It can be a transition state while planning and executing the transformation from APO and ECC to S/4HANA and IBP. 

  • Rapid supply chain transformation without transitioning from ECC to S/4HANA
  • Modular enhancements integrated with ERP core
  • Enable centralized hub scenarios connected to multiple ERPs (both ECC and S/4HANA)

For further information on the potential transition options and deployment scenarios please refer to:
SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, Edition for SAP S/4HANA | SAP Community
Accelerate Supply Chain Transformation and Innovation (sap.com)
SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, Edition for SAP S/4HANA (overview)
SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA | SAP Help Portal

Additional information:
SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling
PP/DS for SAP S/4HANA (Advanced Planning) - SAP Community
Overview of the key functionality of embedded PP/DS on S/4HANA - SAP Community
SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling - Release 2023

Roadmap: SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling (Business Capability: Constraint-Based Production Planning)

SAP Synchronized Planning for Production

Synchronized Planning for Production (‘SYPP’) is not a standalone product, but a closed loop process of supply chain planning and execution working seamlessly across different planning horizons and time buckets. It combines the planning capabilities of SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) for Supply and SAP S/4HANA PP/DS in a synchronized way. SAP IBP translates the customer demand to trigger off the requirements for an efficient manufacturing planning which in turn drives the Detailed Scheduling that are embedded in S/4HANA. The IBP interoperability with PP/DS now offers truly synchronized planning from strategy down to machine level scheduling. SYPP is also available with the standalone edition of PPDS (see above S/4HANA DSC-Edition, side car deployment) offering customers and independent end2end planning environment independent of the ERP backend.

Synchronized Planning for Production is one of the building blocks of SAP’s Synchronized Planning strategy. To find out more about the overall Synchronized Planning strategy look here: Achieving Supply Chain Resiliency by Synchronizing Business Decisions (sap.com).

Synchronized Planning for Production is based on the following solution components:

  • SAP S/4HANA MP&S (PP/DS) and
  • SAP IBP for Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) and/or SAP IBP for Response & Supply

The solution is SAP IBP supply planning with production planning integration to SAP MP&S (PP/DS).

Key capabilities include:

  • Create transparent plans from supply network planning to production planning and detailed scheduling
  • Synchronize your business processes, plans and data - from planning to execution
  • Provide end-to-end visibility and decision support to your planners
  • Order Integration for operational supply planning in real time between SAP IBP and PP/DS in SAP S/4HANA (Figure 5)
  • Key Figure Integration for tactical supply planning by sharing constraints between SAP IBP and PP/DS in SAP S/4HANA

Process scenarios with Order integration


Figure 5 – Process scenarios with Order integration

 

Process scenarios with Key Figure (Time Series) integration


Figure 6 – Process scenarios with Order integration

 

Additional information
Exploring the Benefits of Synchronized Planning fo... - SAP Community (important links to detailed information on the solution, integration scenarios, processes and setup)
Roadmap: SAP Synchronized Planning for Production

 

Criteria to determine the most suitable target solution

Based on the characterization of the solution options SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for Planning and Scheduling (MP&S) is a mandatory component, either the embedded or the side-by-side deployment option. Manufacturing customers with SAP IBP should consider the Synchronized Planning for Production for the additional benefits.

Existing SAP APO customers using APO Supply Network Planning (SNP) and PP/DS can consider Synchronized Planning as a viable alternative to move to S/4HANA and IBP.

Questions that can provide some orientation from Production Planning and Scheduling perspective

  1. Customer runs SAP ECC and SAP APO (PP/DS and/or SNP)
    As the mainstream maintenance is nearing its end consider the Synchronized Planning strategy and plan the transition to S/4HANA and IBP.  As a transition state customer can consider S/4HANA MP&S (PP/DS) side by side deployment.

  2. Customer with multiple ERP systems and an APO system
    Consider the transition scenarios and stages to the final target architecture w/ S/4HANA and IBP. Building a roadmap of exact sequencing and phases need careful evaluation of various factor, e.g., the instance options, business processes and priorities.

  3. Customer runs multiple ERP systems and need for centralized Production Planning system
    Consider SAP MP&S (PP/DS) side car deployment for hub scenario.  

  4. Customer runs SAP IBP
    Existing SAP IBP customer with the need for PP/DS capabilities need to consider the SYPP solution - S/4HANA MP&S (PP/DS) and the integration between IBP supply planning and PP/DS.

  5. Customer runs ECC 
    Existing ECC customer with the need for PP/DS capabilities should plan the transition to S/4HANA with embedded PP/DS and consider adding IBP in a later phase.

 

Decision Tree

A simple decision tree to determine the target application architecture is illustrated below:

 

Conclusion

Companies using SAP ECC and SAP APO for PP/DS need to consider the transition options to SAP S/4HANA with embedded PP/DS and replace APO functionality S/4HANA and SAP IBP. The SAP DSC edition for S/4HANA (side car) offers alternatives for the transition as well as for the final target architecture (hub scenario).

entry.title: Re: Currency Conversion: Different Booking Date for diffe...
entry.content: Okay, thanks for your answer!

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - SAP AI Services
entry.content:
Hi, 

I got nice results. title of the recipe and the ingredients.

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - SAP AI Services
entry.content: Hi @Sabarim_07, Yes the service we are using is for extracting text from documents (pdf or images) and identifying what the test is. So title and ingredients in our example. In business context that could be order number or customer or phone number, email and address, line items, total amount or currency and so on. Therefore, feeding only an image without text does not work with this service. What you are suggesting would be an image recognition and object detection task.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

I hope this message finds you well.

Firstly, I want to express my sincere gratitude for providing us with this wonderful opportunity.

My name is Tuan, and I have been immersed in the IT domain for the past 7 years. Currently, I am employed as an ERP specialist, where I primarily focus on supporting the daily operations of our company's private ERP system. Recently, I have become increasingly interested in SAP and ABAP technologies. Encouraged by this newfound interest, I made the decision to embark on a journey to learn SAP and ABAP. I have successfully completed the course "Acquiring Core ABAP Skills" which I found incredibly insightful. You can find the link to the course here: Acquiring Core ABAP Skills.

My ultimate aspiration is to transition into a role as an ABAP developer. While this transition poses certain challenges, particularly with my wife expecting a baby, I firmly believe that now is the opportune moment to pursue my career aspirations. After all, if not now, then when?

With that said, I kindly request your assistance in obtaining a voucher for a free attempt at the SAP certification exam. This opportunity would mean the world to me and would serve as a crucial first step towards achieving my goals in the SAP ecosystem.

Thank you in advance for your consideration and support.
My email: tuanvst1809@gmail.com

Warm regards,

Tuan

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - SAP AI Services
entry.content: Hi @thomas_mller13, no this service does not use LayoutLM but there are other algorithms based on layout that are being used. Here is a description of the underlying algorithms of the base edition: this “2-min of” video. The premium edition uses GPT in the background to determine all kinds of other values.

entry.title: Re: Action Drop Down Menu feature in Fiori Elements for OData v4
entry.content:
Hello and many thanks for trying out this feature,

I've taken some time to research this, including a review of the relevant documentation. For more detailed information, you might find this link helpful:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAPUI5/96880755e4e64fcd96c12694f430fece/b623e0bbbb2b4147b2d0516c463921a0.html?locale=en-US&state=DRAFT

Regarding the code snippet you've shared, I suggest you also check out this page:
https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/cbf16c599f2d4b8796e3702f7d4aae6c

In the subsection titled 'Grouping Actions as Menu Buttons,' there's an example for an ObjectPage which might be beneficial for you. Given the example you've provided, it would be a good idea to follow that model and give it another try. Please let us know if you need further assistance.

entry.title: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Business Technology Platform – Blog 7
entry.content:
Blog 7: The Role of Automation for Transformation Projects

In times of constant change, quickly emerging demands for digitalization and competitive markets, companies are looking for ways to further optimize their processes. Consequently, many organizations have turned to automation technologies, such as RPA tools, to automate mundane and repetitive tasks. The benefits of automation are quite obvious: Automated processes are faster, less prone to error and people can concentrate on more complex, higher value-added activities.

Especially for activities with high volumes the benefits can be huge. Let me illustrate this with an example: A few weeks ago, I talked to a company from the consumer products industry. While a lot of transactions with its suppliers run fully digital via B2B integration, they still receive roughly 4.000 documents per month such as invoices and purchase orders via email. Let us assume you need on average 2 minutes for processing one document, that is: Extracting and verifying the data, collecting approvals, creating it in the ERP system and so on. In total, this sums up to over 130 hours of manual work per month. Applying RPA tooling and AI for document understanding can drastically speed up the processing of these tasks, thereby freeing people from tedious copy & paste or data entry and export activities.

Why looking at automation only is not enough

To streamline their processes, many companies have also started looking into other technologies: Besides RPA, also workflow engines, integration middleware as well as tools for process mining and process analytics have risen. One big downside is that these technologies often are not well integrated and hence customers are struggling with setting up an aligned end-to-end approach.

With our Enterprise Automation offering we want to address this and provide a unified and interlinked approach. As described in the blog article by Angela Harvey, this concept is based on the combination of 3 products:

  • SAP Integration Suite: You cannot automate what you can’t connect to. Our suite provides unparalleled connectivity and integration for a variety of scenarios. Be it SAP / Non-SAP, Cloud / on-Premise applications, B2B, B2G, API-led or event-driven scenarios.
  • SAP Signavio: Before you optimize, you might need to understand where to start and uncover inefficiencies first. With SAP Signavio you can analyze, model and govern your business processes to drive operational change.
  • SAP Build: Drive automation of long-running processes with capabilities for app development, process automation and portal sites. Low-Code/No-Code and visual design tooling also empower citizen developers and business experts to contribute to the development process in an agile fashion.

It is important to note that Enterprise Automation is a flexible and modular concept. Hence, customers can start with whatever product they see fit. From a transformation perspective Enterprise Automation can provide significant benefits when modernizing your SAP ERP, so when moving from the classic SAP Business Suite to SAP S/4HANA. It can be applied at any stage, both before the migration as well as after the migration:

  • Before the move: Leverage Signavio’s “plug and gain” approach and LeanIX to analyze and clean up processes in advance and gain immediate benefits from applying automation (e.g. via pre-built content packages). Use the Integration Solution Advisory Methodology of the SAP Integration Suite to design and prepare a future-proof integration strategy.
  • After the move: Bring process excellence to the next level by combining process mining, integration and automation and enable a continuous improvement cycle. Enable a governed approach to accelerate and scale the building of process automations based on Low-Code/No-Code techniques.

Another advantage in terms of transformation is the versatility – similar to a Swiss Army Knife. Enterprise Automation allows process optimization across the enterprise and line of businesses: Be it sales, procurement, HR, finance, production or logistics. A comprehensive collection of use cases and end-to-end scenarios has been put together by Manuel Namyslo in his blog article: How to get started with Enterprise Automation.

The role of AI in Enterprise Automation

Let us take a closer look at the role of AI and how it fits into the picture of Enterprise Automation. When transforming business processes and modernizing enterprise applications, AI should be part of the agenda, due to the massive benefits it can provide. Within our Enterprise Automation offering, the AI capabilities are tightly embedded to make the consumption as easy, seamless and relevant as possible.

A few highlights of them include:

  • Use Document Information Extraction (DOX) within SAP Build Process Automation to grab data from documents like invoices, payment advices and purchase orders to automate their processing. We also plan to integrate the DOX premium edition to make the processing of arbitrary documents with varying structure and layout even simpler.
  • We plan to use AI to provide recommendations for workflows as proposals based on historic data. This will support and speed up the decision making for approvals and workflow items (see roadmap item).
  • Visual tools already cater towards ease-of-use and simplify the building of process automations by business experts. There are also plans to make the design process even more intuitive by leveraging Generative AI functions (see corresponding roadmap items). As illustrated in this preview video, users describe what they need or what they want to craft in natural language. This can be an entire process flow, but also single process steps such as a business rule. The system then automatically generates the respective building blocks. Apart from that the AI-based assistant will also help with the discovery of pre-built content by providing suitable proposals from the content catalog.

As you can see AI is a crucial factor in many ways. Not only for the automation of processes, but also to accelerate the design and development process in various aspects. In addition, Enterprise Automation can also help with infusing AI into the process layer, by providing the respective integration capabilities.

To conclude: Enterprise Automation should be considered as a strategic tool set in the light of any transformation initiative for ERP and enterprise application landscapes. Its modular style allows you to start small at any stage of your project: From simple workflow automation and high-level process modelling, up to a fully-fledged vehicle for continuous process excellence. While our offering is optimized for SAP applications and systems, we embrace openness and enable process excellence across heterogeneous IT landscapes.

Where to look next and where to start? I recommend checking out the blog article How to get started with Enterprise Automation to get further inspiration. In addition, Low-Code/No-Code tooling such as SAP Build is well suited for getting Hands-On experience. Therefore, feel free to reach out to your SAP account manager to experience the ease-of-use in a Hands-On workshop. Finally, start by looking into potential use cases and assess them with the help of SAP or partners on how to tackle them with Enterprise Automation.

Previous Blogs:

  • Blog 1: The Central Role of Clean Core and AI
  • Blog 2: The Role of BTP as Extension and Innovation Platform
  • Blog 3: How Clean Core can accelerate your SAP Business Transformation
  • Blog 4: Mastering the Data Dance: The Potential of Clean Core and AI
  • Blog 5: How hybrid Integration upholds the role of Clean Core
  • Blog 6: Clean Core Extensibility

entry.title: How to create New Account group in SAP Public cloud We are getting below error
entry.content:

ount group in SAP Public cloud We are getting below error

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Replication Flow - Started failing w...
entry.content:
Hi Mandar,

the root cause cannot be tracked down by this error message.
You can check the application log in SLG1 on the source S/4 HANA System (Log Object DH*), if you get further errors that help you to understand the problem.
Otherwise please raise a ticket on CA-DI-IS-ABA and let the SAP support check.

Best Regards,

Nico

entry.title: Re: Monitoring Apps on Kyma
entry.content:
Hi Sarbajeet,

You can leverage SAP Cloud Logging Service. Please refer to blogs for further information:

- https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/kyma-integration-with-sap-cloud-logging-part-1-introduction-and-shipping/ba-p/13648649

- https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/kyma-integration-with-sap-cloud-logging-part-2-let-s-ship-some-traces/ba-p/13674600

This is a managed service from SAP, which means less overhead on the customer side.

 

However, if you would prefer to use an open source tool, Kyma Open Telemetry supports integration with Prometheus stack too as well as with other open source tools that support open telemetry protocol. Please check this example for using the kube prometheus stack:
- https://github.com/kyma-project/examples/tree/main/prometheus 

Please note that in this case, the customer will be managing the Prometheus stack.

BR

Gaurav

entry.title: Re: Authentication for e2e Test Automation on BTP with SA...
entry.content:
To give everyone discovering this question a quick update.

In order to have a sign in with a gui to automate in wdi5 I set up a new authentication provider with https://auth0.com/

There is a tutorial setting up auth0 for social sign ins which I roughly followed https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/auth0-log-in-to-btp-with-your-social-accounts/ba-p/13518400

Now I am stuck at fine tuning the sign in to allow authentication via auth0 for Build and Workzone, but I guess this is more of a btp issue.

entry.title: SAP Build FTP Get File Collection Issue
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm encountering an issue in SAP Build when attempting to retrieve a File Collection from SFTP (Port 22). Although the connection appears to be stable and the folder path is valid, when utilizing the Get File Collection for FTP function, it returns 0 lists and 0 elements, despite the presence of multiple files within the folder.

entry.title: Re: Is activation of US language a requirement/prerequisi...
entry.content:
Language selection is relevant for the end user UIs of the Identity Services as well as emails being sent and - if configured - terms-of-use and privacy documents.

see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/supported-languages and https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-default-language-for-end-user-screens for details which languages are supported and how default language is determined. 

One should setup those languages which are relevant for users being authenticated and registered in the Identity Services, but it is not mandatory - fallback/default language is always en-us.

entry.title: How to access an On premise odata service in SAP BTP
entry.content:
I've an se38 report as well as an odata service for the product. 

My requirement is to access the fully developed abap project (in on-premise hana system) via SAP BTP.

Can I push the project into cloud foundry, if possible, pls share the procedure to do so. If there is an alternative means by which we can reach the product on the cloud, pls share that too.

entry.title: Re: How to get output channel of a billing output form in...
entry.content: Hey, I am also having an almost similar issue. Did you find any solution?

entry.title: Passing Internal Table / Deep Structure in Submit program
entry.content:
In the world of ABAP development, we often encounter the need to pass data between programs. Recently, I faced a requirement to transfer an internal table from one report program to another. After extensive research, I realized there wasn’t a straightforward method available online. Therefore, I devised a solution that involves converting the table data into JSON format and passing it as a string parameter to the called program, which then converts it back into a table. I believe this approach could be beneficial to others, and here’s how I implemented it:

In the Caller Program:

  1. Convert the Internal Table to JSON:

    DATA(lv_json) TYPE string.
lv_json = /ui2/cl_json=>serialize( data = lt_data ).

  2. Pass the JSON Data to the Called Program:

    SUBMIT z_submit WITH p_data = lv_json
  AND RETURN.

In the Called Program (z_submit):

  1. Create a Report with a String Parameter:

    REPORT z_submit.
PARAMETERS: p_data TYPE string.

  2. Define the Table Type: (Here, you would define the structure of your internal table/ Deep Structure that matches the JSON data structure.)

  3. Convert JSON Data (String Parameter) Back to Internal Table:

    DATA: lt_data TYPE <your_table_type>.
/ui2/cl_json=>deserialize(
  EXPORTING
    json = p_data
  CHANGING
    data = lt_data
).

This method is particularly useful when dealing with large datasets that need to be passed between programs without the use of global variables or database persistence. It’s a clean and efficient way to serialize and deserialize data.

I hope this technique proves to be useful. If you have any insights or know of a more straightforward method, please feel free to share your thoughts!

#abap

entry.title: Hi Whats does this mean Node: "Corporate-data-model" Error : Element name not defined in DTD
Question about sap fiori elements list report using odata v2 and odata v4
entry.content:
Hello experts, I have a question about building applications using the list report in SAP Fiori Elements with odata v2 and odata v4. When I create a project with odata v4, it does not create the CRUD structure automatically and neither the creation of the list nor the edit object page

 

So I decided to try with odata v2 and if the crud is created automatically but the page map stops working for some things like the creation of sections in the object header, the + does not appear to add one

The ones that are created are through annotations but I would like to use the page map the same as in odata v4 or, failing that, have odata v4 create the entire crud for me.

thanks for your help

Best regards,
Santiago Castano

entry.title: Re: Advanced Financial Closing in SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hello,

in your back end configuration for SAP Advanced Financial Closing we have got only task type "Fiori-Web Application". I works for both, Fiori and Gui Transaction Code. As soon as you do not want to use Fiori you need to copy the Business Transaction Type into Custom Namespace (Y* or Z*) and delete the information about Sematic Tag and Semantic Action as this is navigating into Fiori Launchpad. Type in the transaction code instead in column for transaction code. End user will open Gui for HTML instead of Fiori now.

Kind Regards,

Christian

entry.title: Re: How to query for bin location/warehouse history for i...
entry.content: In addition to that it was very helpful to have a look at the definition of the view OINM. It shows how a join from TransSeq to MessageID is supposed to look.

entry.title: Custom BP roles and Partner Role for Pricing
entry.content:
Hi,

My customer need to manage a custom field in the BP linked to the Sold-to, but from the moment that he has requested to manage the validity date, I've thought to manage it by relationship tab in which we identify the Payer linked to the Sold-to with the start and end validity date. The point is that we need to link the custom field to the pricing condition, so I've thought to create a BP custom role and a partner custom role in way that when I'll create the pricing condition, I will create for Sales Area and partner custom role created, but I am not sure that these steps enable to read the validity date on the relationship tab by the standard and it works.

Example: The custom field is called "category 1" with start validity date 01.01.2020 and end validity date 31.02.2021. From the date 01.03.2021 the sold-to change to "category 2", so I need to say at the system the following:

From the date 01.03.2021 give me the pricing condition for the "category 2" and not more "category 1". How I can do that? Any idea?

Thanks for the help!

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
UweFetzer_se38

Hi Uwe, 

Very sorry to hear.

If this happens again, please let us know.

Best wishes,

Julie Plummer.

entry.title: ABAP RAP: Create with dynamic Facets
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I saw at FioriConf that it is now possible to implement dynamic facets in create mode. This means that the facets change with a certain selection, for example through a dropdown in the first facet. Unfortunately, I can't find any documentation on this topic.

I currently have a case of a more complex transaction that was solved in SAPUI5 using the wizard. Maintaining the whole thing is of course very time-consuming and RAP and Fiori Elements are actually the way to go for us. The advantage of the wizard is of course that I can go many different ways depending on the selection. So the form is very dynamic.

Examples that are possible with the wizard:

A -> B -> B1 -> B2 -> C -> D

A -> B -> C -> D

A -> C -> D

Perhaps someone can show me an example of how I can implement dynamic facets in create mode. And how far can the whole thing be taken? Compared to the Wizard.

Best regards
Lars

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration

hi Alex,

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-pipeline-concept-and-b2b-tpm-testing/ba-p/13691706

Best Regards,

Michal Krawczyk

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
@alejiandro_sensejl 

Hi Alejiandro,

This is the official answer:
"

The SAP Managed tag page the user linked to below, only displays a small snapshot of all content with any given tag. We recommend our users subscribe to our RSS feeds if they would like to be notified of all content for specific tags. We have more information here: https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/exploring-the-rss-feeds-of-the-new-sap-community-platform/ba-p/13594677

and here: https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/additional-sap-community-feeds/ba-p/13615436"

Best wishes,

Julie Plummer.

I hope you can realize your plan to unite the SAP Community within the Baltic. 🤞

entry.title: SAP Business One Mobile app, SAP Business One Service and SAP Business One Sales mobile app
entry.content:
Hi,

I tried to access the 'Demo' of the SAP Business One Mobile App, SAP Business One Sales App, and SAP Business One Service App, but was not able to access them and encountered errors. See the attached error messages for the respective apps. I have tested since last week but have never had a chance to be able to access it. Could it be that the that the SAP server for the mobile apps is down?

SAP Business One Mobile App error: Permission Denied. I'm accessing the SAP Demo system, it does not required any special setup, for the past I can access without issue, why now prompt error 'Permission Denied'?

SAP Business One Service App error: Log in failed. I'm accessing the SAP Demo system, it does not required any special setup, for the past I can access without issue, why now prompt error 'Log in failed'?

SAP Business One Sales App error: Log in failed. I'm accessing the SAP Demo system, it does not required any special setup, for the past I can access without issue, why now why prompt error 'Log in failed'?

entry.title: Re: Vectorize your Data : SAP HANA Cloud's Vector Engine for Unified Data Excellence
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

Can we vectorize text data to embeddings in hana cloud only. 

Regards

Ashoo Pal

entry.title: How to get output channel of a billing output form in S4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello,


Developed a custom driver program that calls a custom Adobe form, to generate the billing output form.
All the configurations are done in BRF+ and OPD and everything runs smoothly until there pop up the request to attach this document with the billing document.

In my driver program after I call the functions
FP_JOB_OPEN.
The function that generates the form
FP_JOB_CLOSE

I have implemented a perform called ATTACH_PDF.

but when in the BRF+ the billing output gets mapped for 2 channels
PRINT
EMAIL
the attach perform gets called twice since the driver program runs twice, once for each channel.

Is there any class/method/ FM that I can call so that I can get as a response the channel of this output, so I can put the ATTACH_PDF perform inside an IF statement and execute it only on the print channel???

Thanks in advance.

Himanshu Kawatra

SD Billing SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP for SAP Business Suite 
SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP  SAP Forms service by Adobe

entry.title: Re: New Card Upload Process and Role-based Filtering of Cards in Work Zone Adv
entry.content:
I guess this whole operation is being done to move away from content stored inside of WZ and migrate to HTML5 repo.  Is there a way to get access to the card content via the "cf html5-list" CLI?

 

---

FYI - it's good to know that when you delete the card from the Admin Console and recreate it via the Content Manager, you will not lose the assignments or configuration of the card! 😉

entry.title: Re: Failed to load reported financial data with document...
entry.content:
Hi Derek.

Yes, that correct that the Data Coll. app does not support DT 00.

To upload DT 00 you need to use the Flexible Upload App (see attached) and use the template. 

Br.

Jan

entry.title: Direct filters with linked dimensions on date vs calendar week
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have two import models in the SAC with linked dimensions. One of the linked dimensions is the date. But in the story we have set the date-filter on calendar week and not the specific date in day granularity as we want to have a look at whole calendar weeks.

So what I am wondering now is: What does the SAC do in the background?

=> When I am filtering in the primary model on a certain calendar week with the calendar week filter in the story all data is being selected with a date within the selected calendar week. That is clear and easy to understand.

=> ...but how  does the direct filter via the linked dimension select on the secondary model and its date dimension in detail? Will the selction of the whole calendar week on the primary model be forwarded to the secondary model? Or will it just be the dates on day granularity found in the primary model that will be forwarded to the secondary model?

For example:
When I would select on calendar week 18 in 20024 and I only have data in there for Monday and Tuesday ( 2024/04/29 and 2024/04/30) but in the secondary model is data for each day of the week, will the secondary model just be filtered with 2024/04/29 and 2024/04/30 (and the data of 2024/05/01, ... 2024/05/05 will be missing) or will it be filtered with all days / dates of calendar week 18.2024?

Thanks a lot!
Martin

entry.title: Re: Maintain the conversion factor for the alternative unit KG in Bapi
entry.content: Hi, did you ever find the solution? I am having the same issues...

entry.title: SAP LMD/DSD - Last Mile Distribution
entry.content:
Following up to my last blog  SAP TM Integration with Last Mile Distribution - SAP Community will get in details in this blog I will focus on Route Execution.

There is different route type dependence how you setup your scenarios, for example, presales, delivery, or van sales. However, it is also possible to define route types for routes that combine the planning and execution of deliveries, van sales, and presales or merchandiser activities. For example, a delivery driver who typically delivers goods to customers might also occasionally capture sales orders and returns for the next delivery cycle.

You define a Route type with the build sequence.

After defining the Route follow the next path:

  • Map Source Types to Route Types
  • Map Source Statuses to Route Life Cycle Statuses
  • Map Route Categories to Route Types

More details can be check here: Route | SAP Help Portal

A Route exists 2 types with SAP LMD:

  • Paper-Based: All documents related to a route are printed out for the delivery driver. During route execution, the driver records all activities on paper. After a route is completed, the driver hands in all remaining documents and a settlement clerk checks all route information and records it by using the Settle Route Data app.
  • Mobile: All information related to a route is downloaded to the mobile device of the driver. During route execution, the driver records all activities on the mobile device. The driver uploads information about individual stops either during route execution or on completion of the route.

SAP LMD have a standard mobile solution which the Driver can use for the Last Mile Distribution.

SAP Direct Distribution - A New Mobile App for Dir... - SAP Community

3098683 - Last Mile Distribution Mobile Adapter: Mobile Application Configuration Handling - SAP for Me

Mobile Application SAP_LMD | SAP Help Portal

In this scenario will use the Standard Fiori App (Settle Route Data) to the Route Execution:

Once you open the Settle Route Data, can be filter with some parameters.

The Route ID in standard is the same number as the FO number from TM.

When we get inside in The Route, we can see the sequence of the process:

  • Check Out
  • Customer Documents
  • Check In
  • Payments
  • Differences
  • Log
  • Comments

In each of those Tab we can see the details of the Goods + Quantities the driver is moving for each customer and of the total are loading in the vehicle.

In the Check Out before the Driver leave the Warehouse the quantities can be adjusts in case is needed and following the Reason Code and Inventory Group.

In the next blog will follow with the Route Execution and Release for Settlement.

Refer:

Getting Started with Last Mile Distribution | SAP Help Portal

Route | SAP Help Portal

Execution of Delivery Routes | SAP Help Portal

Integration with Transportation Management | SAP Help Portal

SAP Direct Distribution | SAP Help Portal

3358604 - Last Mile Distribution - Release information/restriction note for OP2023 FPS0 - SAP for Me

Thanks and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP - Access Request Body in error handler for 40...
entry.content:
Hi @Arley , thank you, this works fine for entity served as projection of the ones in the data-model.cds.

In the scenario in which I am exposing an action with a custom defined type, the cds.context.http.req.data it is still undefined. Any suggestions?

 

 

 

define type EventMessage {
        ID       :      UUID;
        time     :      DateTime;
        name     :      String;
        category :      String;
        status   :      String;

    }
... 

action add @(restrict: [
        {
            grant: ['WRITE'],
            to   : 'admin'
        }])(
            ID   : EventMessage:ID,
            time : EventMessage:time,
            name : EventMessage:name,
            category : EventMessage:category,
            status : EventMessage:status
            ) returns String;

 

 

--- EDIT

Solved, in this scenario I can access to the body with cds.context.http.req.body

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Passing Internal Table / Deep Structure in Submit program
entry.content:
That is a nice trick indeed. However I think that if you need to use it, the design of the report being submitted is flawed. 

Selection parameters are meant to be filled by end users and no end user is able to enter a JSON string manually. 

A simpler approach would be to use EXPORT TO MEMORY ID / IMPORT FROM MEMORY ID statements.

entry.title: Re: Production documents not imported - SFM
entry.content:
Hi,

Here you find a mapping list of Master Data Entities:
https://help.sap.com/docs/sustainability-footprint-management/application-help-for-corporate-and-products/master-data-entities

Production documents point to the AUFK (Order master data) table.

If you click on the line it should give you a more detailed error.

Cheers,
Dietmar

entry.title: Re: Failed to load reported financial data with document type 00 in apps data monitor
entry.content:
Hi Derek,

Can you share the header of your upload file?

Br

Martin Jul

Florian

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Certification badge
entry.content:
Hi @Flo08 

Thank you for bringing this to our attention. In order to provide individual support please create a ticket in our User Support Center
To help the support team to understand your query better, could you please provide the following information in your correspondence?

  •            Detailed description of the issue
  •            Screenshots: To help us visualize the issue
  •            Please also provide the Course Code of course.

Kind regards,

Margit

entry.title: Different exchange rate in RFUMSV00
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

we have to set a different exchange rate for RFUMVSA00/VAT Report. Journal entries are translated with exchange rate "M". But for the VAT report, the exchange rate given from the Bundesfinanzministerium (https://www.bundesfinanzministerium.de/Web/DE/Themen/Steuern/Steuerarten/Umsatzsteuer/Umsatzsteuer_Umrechnungskurse/umsatzsteuer_umrechnungskurse.html) should be used.

Is this possible in a public cloud system? And if yes, what SSCUI´s do I have to customize to get the desired report?

Best Regards and Thank you for any hints,

Sabrina

Regards

entry.title: Re: Sending ASN from Freight Order (embedded TM)
entry.content:
Hello thomaspasberg,

thanks a lot for your response. We don't use ASR. We don't see any advantage using this scenario.

My understanding is that we have to substitute the current shipment (LE-TRA) where we send the DESADV with a freight order in SAP-TM (embedded) sending the ASN-message. Furthermore, it should be possible to send it from a freight order and the consignment order is NOT mandatory. Please correct me, if I should be wrong.

We try to set-up the message with:

Is this correct?

Unfortunately, there is no "how-to" and/or specific guideline how to set-up the ASN. This would be very helpful for customers moving from LE-TRA to SAP-TM.

Regards,
Thorsten

entry.title: Approach to identify and remove duplicates from SAP ECC & CRM applications
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We would like to know if there is any tool or methodology which could identify and cleanup duplicate data in CRM and ERP application such as contacts, and customers. We have a client which is currently on SAP stack and want to accelerate the data cleanup process from CRM and ERP applications. If you have developed any programs, tools, or processes which can be used then we would love to leverage those instead of manual workaround.

Regards,

Deepak Rastogi

entry.title: Re: Certification: Discovering SAP Activate - Implementation Tools and Methodology
entry.content:

We received your request and are in the process of evaluating the problem.
Please allow for a delay in our response.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Need clarification on systems for Snote implementation & Managed System configuration.
entry.content:
Hello SAP,
We have below concerns/queries:
1) Is Security S-notes implementation required in (SAP Cloud Connector) and (SAP Convergent Mediation) systems? If yes, please let us know how to implement S-notes.
2) Is it possible to do "Managed Systems Configuration" for these 2 systems in production solman system?
Could you please clarify on these two concerns/queries.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need clarification on systems for Snote implementatio...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Regarding your concerns, 
1. Security notes implementations are required in various SAP systems, so it should be the same for your systems. 
Please refer to How to Implement SAP note in SNOTE - SAP Community for implementing SAP note in SNOTE. Should you have other concerns regarding note implementation, please contact component BC-UPG-NA for further support.
2. It is possible to do managed system configuration for these systems in solution manager.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Certification: Discovering SAP Activate - Implementat...
entry.content: 🙂

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report / PrintOutputController.ModifyPrinterN...
entry.content:
Hello Gottfried,

we experience this problem with different printer manufacturers (e.g. Sharp, UTAX and Kyocera also). Sharp told us to use an universal driver. 😠
We found that this problem only occurs when printing to a problematic printer that is not the default printer. It is not a solution, but perhaps a viable workaround, to make it the default printer.

Regards
HML

Currently there are no live sessions planned for this topic.

Margit
